steps.title: Use the root exponent rule
steps.content:
A fractional exponent with numerator $1$ means a root. The denominator of the exponent tells which root to use:
$$x^{\frac{1}{n}}=\sqrt[n]{x}$$

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
Here, the denominator is $13$, so the expression represents the thirteenth root of $x$:
$$x^{\frac{1}{13}}=\sqrt[13]{x}$$The answer is A.
